name: Pluto
summary: Pluto is a dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt. Once classified as the ninth planet, it has a complex surface with nitrogen and methane ices, mountains, and a thin atmosphere. Its largest moon, Charon, is large enough that Pluto–Charon are often considered a binary system.

name: Eris
summary: Eris is a massive scattered-disk object and one of the most massive known dwarf planets. Its discovery was a major driver in the redefinition of 'planet' by the IAU in 2006.

name: Haumea
summary: Haumea is a rapidly rotating, elongated dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt, notable for its high spin rate and collisional family.

name: Makemake
summary: Makemake is a bright Kuiper Belt object covered in methane ices. It has one small known moon and was discovered in 2005.

name: Ceres
summary: Ceres is the largest object in the asteroid belt and the only dwarf planet in the inner Solar System. Spacecraft observations show a differentiated interior and localized bright spots (salt deposits) on its surface.
